---
title: "Join us"
---

New tools are producing brain data at a scale and quality we have never had before, and with it the chance to answer long-standing questions about how the brain computes. It is a good time for quantitative scientists to move into biology. We welcome people from physics, mathematics, computer science, and neuroscience who want to bring quantitative rigor to these questions.

We are recruiting at every level and offer a collaborative, supportive environment:

- **Postdocs** building mechanistic models from whole-brain connectomes
- **PhD students** who want to rotate and learn to analyze state-of-the-art datasets
- **Undergraduates** seeking hands-on experience in programming and data analysis; we ask for a six-month minimum so there is time to train properly

To apply, email me with your CV and a short description of your research interests and background—quantitative coursework, coding experience, or previous research. Postdoctoral candidates should also include contact details for two or three references. Prospective graduate students must apply through the university's graduate program, but are encouraged to reach out first to discuss projects.
